title: eriogonum mohavense subsp. ampullaceum
intro: Eriogonum mohavense subsp. ampullaceum, also known as Mojave buckwheat, is a plant species in the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae) that is native to the Mojave Desert in California and Nevada. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum mohavense subsp. ampullaceum include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and promoting skin health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and grinding the plant material to make a powder for topical application.
